title: Madhuca rupicola
intro: Madhuca rupicola, also known as the Himalayan mahua, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Himalayas, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of M. rupicola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is commonly used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Madhuca rupicola is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is a tree native to South America. Further classification of Madhuca rupicola includes its genus being placed in the subfamily Chrysophoroideae. This classification places it within the larger family Sapotaceae, which includes numerous other tropical tree species. The exact classification of Madhuca rupicola may require further research for precise details.
